retrofit_needed.sibling_chip_nav_update: Arkansas sibling chip-navs should be cross-checked to ensure 'Worker classification' entry points to this page using the row-slug convention.

notes: Programmatic US-state child page for Arkansas worker classification. Mirrors structural sibling at ../../arizona/worker-classification-state-test/. Lead angle: Arkansas codified the IRS 20-factor test via the Empower Independent Contractors Act of 2019 (Act 1055; Ark. Code § 11-1-204) and applied it uniformly across all five state-law purposes (UI, workers' comp, state tax, wage and hour, equal pay). This is the strongest Arkansas angle — a uniformity claim that competitors typically miss. The sprint prompt initially understated Arkansas's framework as 'no state-specific ABC test, IRS common-law' — verified statutory research found the 2019 codification makes Arkansas stronger and more distinctive than that framing suggested. Income tax rate corrected mid-build from 3.9% to 3.7% per the sibling agent's update to arkansas.json (SB1/HB1001 cut signed 6 May 2026, retroactive to 1 January 2026). The original sprint prompt's reference to '§ 11-10-721' and a '$5,000 per violation' penalty was not verifiable from primary sources; replaced with verified DWS guidance (§ 11-10-519, plus DWS rate-manipulation 10% penalty + 2% rate increase rule). Hero photo: Arkansas State Capitol, Little Rock (Jametlene Reskp via Unsplash, 115KB).
